title: hypericum ellipticum
intro: Hypericum ellipticum, also known as tutsan, is a perennial shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its elliptical leaves and small, yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Hypericum ellipticum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Hypericum ellipticum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or ointment, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
